2606.09251.title: TruthSplit: Operationalizing Conditional Validity in Arguments Through Multi-Perspective Reasoning
2606.09251.paper_summary: We present TruthSplit, an interactive system for multi-perspective argument analysis. Existing argumentation tools typically analyze properties of the argument itself, such as structure, quality, stance, or persuasiveness, while leaving perspective-specific background knowledge implicit. TruthSplit addresses this gap by supporting an exploratory analysis of how the same claim can lead to different conclusions when interpreted through worldview-specific values, assumptions, and conceptual definitions. We refer to this perspective-dependent analysis as conditional validity. Given an input argumentative text, TruthSplit extracts claims and premises, applies a three-layer natural language inference (NLI) approach to assess both logical and worldview-specific normative consistency, and conditions large language model (LLM) reasoning on structured worldview profiles that encode core values and decision principles. The system then generates perspective-specific interpretations, identifies value conflicts and assumption gaps, and visualizes divergence through interactive analytical interfaces.
2606.09251.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 TruthSplit，一個用於多角度論證分析的互動系統。現有的論證工具通常分析論證本身的屬性，例如結構、質量、立場或說服力，而將特定視角的背景知識隱含。TruthSplit 通過支持探索性分析，填補了這一空白，分析相同的主張如何在通過特定世界觀的價值觀、假設和概念定義進行解釋時導致不同的結論。我們將這種依賴視角的分析稱為條件有效性。給定一個輸入的論證文本，TruthSplit 提取主張和前提，應用三層自然語言推理（NLI）方法來評估邏輯和世界觀特定的規範一致性，並將大型語言模型（LLM）的推理條件化於編碼核心價值觀和決策原則的結構化世界觀檔案。然後，系統生成特定於視角的解釋，識別價值衝突和假設差距，並通過互動分析界面可視化分歧。
2606.09251.author: Benjamin Stieger et.al.
2606.09251.authors: Benjamin Stieger, Maximilian Terberger, Thomas Huber, Christina Niklaus
